data.title: Investors in Vestis Corporation urged to contact Levi & Korsinsky regarding class action securities lawsuit by August 8, 2025 - VSTS (NYSE)
data.description: On August 8, 2025, Levi & Korsinsky, LLP has issued a notification to investors holding shares in Vestis Corporation (NYSE: VSTS) regarding a class action securities lawsuit. The class in question includes Vestis investors who suffered losses due to alleged adverse actions by the company. The lawsuit aims to secure compensation for these investors who were negatively affected.

data.title: 2024 saw highest assaults on police officers in a decade, FBI reports. National Police Association backs Back the Blue Act 2025.
data.description: In 2024, the Federal Bureau of Investigation reported the highest rate of assaults on police officers in the past decade in the United States. This alarming trend continued throughout the year, highlighting a concerning increase in violence against law enforcement. Additionally, between 2021 and 2024, there was a significant rise in the number of officers feloniously targeted, indicating a growing threat to the safety and security of police personnel. These statistics underscore the dangers faced by law enforcement officers on duty and emphasize the need for enhanced measures to protect them while carrying out their duties.

data.title: Terri Ross addresses safety and regulatory gaps in booming medical aesthetics industry in two new podcast episodes.
data.description: On August 8, 2025, in Beverly Hills, California, the medical aesthetics sector is experiencing significant growth. However, the lack of stringent regulations and safety supervision is putting both patients and providers at risk. Two informative episodes of the popular podcast "In Touch with Terri: Trade Secrets for..." delve into this issue.

data.title: North America Automotive Robotics Market to reach $7.09 billion by 2033, growing at 9.81% CAGR from 2025 to 2033. Automation demand and AI advancements driving expansion.
data.description: A new report titled "North America Automotive Robotics Market Size and Growth Trends and Forecast Report 2025-2033" has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The market in North America is projected to grow significantly, with expectations to reach US$ 7.09 billion by 2033 compared to US$ 3.05 billion in 2024. This growth represents a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 9.81% from 2025 to 2033. Factors driving this growth include the increasing demand for automation, advancements in Artificial Intelligence (AI) and machine learning technologies, cost savings from reduced labor expenses, improved production efficiency, and the adoption of Industry 4.0 practices.

data.title: AWAKE Chocolate introduces caffeinated products in U.S. Costco stores, expanding retail footprint.
data.description: A popular functional chocolate company, AWAKE Chocolate, has revealed its plans to broaden its retail availability by entering Costco stores. The expansion aims to offer Costco members access to the brand's renowned caffeinated chocolate treats. The company, located in Mississauga, Ontario, shared this development on August 8, 2025. AWAKE Chocolate is known for its delightful array of caffeinated chocolate bites and bars, catering to consumers seeking a flavorful and energizing snack.

data.title: REMAX demonstrates agent-centric innovations at conference, highlighting faster innovation pace in real estate.
data.description: In a demonstration of accelerated innovation, REMAX is setting a new standard in the industry with their latest strategies and energy initiatives. This initiative was showcased during a three-day event in Denver on August 8, 2025. As the leading name in real estate, REMAX brought together Broker/Owners from their extensive network to explore the possibilities, emphasizing the company's commitment to progress.

data.title: Sonny's BBQ hires Matt Zeiger as Chief Financial & Development Officer to drive growth. #leadership #growth #Sonny'sBBQ
data.description: Sonny's BBQ, renowned for its quality food and commitment to spreading kindness, has welcomed Matt Zeiger as the Chief Financial & Development Officer. This move comes as part of the company's strategic growth and efforts to enhance franchise support. Zeiger brings significant expertise to the team and will play a key role in steering the brand forward. The addition of this seasoned leader reinforces Sonny's commitment to expansion and success in the industry. This development was announced on August 8, 2025, in Orlando, Florida.

data.title: Economist warns of stagflation in US economy, predicting dollar fall and inflation rise. Tariffs to impact economy.
data.description: Economist Savvas Savouri is cautioning that the U.S. economy is at risk of stagflation, a scenario where inflation and economic stagnation occur simultaneously. This could have significant consequences for the dollar, bonds, and stocks. Savouri, who serves as the managing director of QuantMetriks, has predicted a future where inflation will increase, the dollar will depreciate, and the yield curve will steepen. The basis for his forecasts lies in three primary factors driving inflation: a weakening dollar, restrictions on inexpensive migrant labor, and heightened tariffs. Savouri believes that the full impact of tariffs is yet to be reflected in the current data. "By looking ahead rather than relying on past data, it becomes evident that stagflation looms over the U.S. economy," he emphasized. Additional details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Celanese stock Q2 earnings expected at $1.40 per share, revenue at $2.49B. Offers 0.25% yield, quarterly dividend of 3 cents per share. How to earn $500 a month from Celanese stock.
data.description: A report on Celanese Corporation (NYSE: CE) is set to be announced after the closing bell on Monday, Aug. 11 for the second quarter earnings. Analysts are expecting the Irving, Texas-based company to reveal that quarterly earnings have decreased to $1.40 per share, down from $2.38 per share in the same period last year. Revenue for this quarter is projected to be $2.49 billion, lower than the $2.65 billion recorded a year ago. Currently, Celanese offers an annual dividend yield of 0.25%, with a quarterly dividend amount of 3 cents per share (equating to 12 cents annually). Interested investors are eyeing potential gains from the company's dividends. To reach a monthly target of $500 from Celanese, investors could start with the yearly goal of $6,000 ($500 x 12 months). For further details, the full article is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: XRP surges 8% amid Ripple-SEC settlement, hitting $3.31 with $196.8B market cap. Traders anticipate rally.
data.description: XRP experienced an 8% rally in response to the resolution of a prolonged legal dispute between Ripple and the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. This event prompted significant trading activity, with many traders viewing it as a pivotal moment for the token. The latest cryptocurrency ticker prices and market caps are as follows: XRP at $3.31 with a market cap of $196.8 billion (up by 11.3%), Bitcoin at $116,643.75 with a market cap of $2.3 trillion (up by 1.2%), and Ethereum at $3,905.11 with a market cap of $471.6 billion (up by 7.3%). Notably, crypto trader Galaxy predicts that XRP surpassing $3.30 is a positive sign and indicates the continuation of an upward trend. For more details, the complete article is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Hudson Global reports 2025 Q2 revenue of $35.5M, a decrease of 0.5% from last year. Net loss is $0.7M. Hudson made investments totaling $1.4M for sales, marketing, and technology enhancements. Cash at $17.5M. Asia Pacific sees a revenue decrease of 3%, EMEA increased by 6%. Share repurchase program in effect with $2.1M remaining. Hudson had $240M NOL as of Dec 31, 2024. The Company will conduct a conference call on August 8, 2025, one of many moves planned for company growth.
data.description: Hudson Global, Inc. reported financial results for the second quarter ended June 30, 2025. The company posted a revenue of $35.5 million, a decrease of 0.5% from Q2 2024. Adjusted net revenue was $18.6 million, up by 5.8% from Q2 2024. Net loss was $0.7 million, with adjusted net income per diluted share at $0.12. Adjusted EBITDA stood at $1.3 million. The company had total cash, including restricted cash, of $17.5 million. In Q2 2025, America's revenue was $7.1 million, Asia Pacific revenue was $21.6 million, and EMEA revenue was $6.8 million. Divulging a dividend of $5 million, the company had purchased 175,041 shares worth $2.9 million, with $2.1 million remaining. Hudson Global has usable net operating losses of $240 million as of December 31, 2024. The company also announced a merger agreement with Star Equity Holdings, expected to enhance shareholder value. Hudson Global plans to continue investments in sales, marketing, and technology to drive future growth. Detailed financial statements for Q2 2025 are available in the article.

data.title: Standard Lithium achieved milestones for SWA Project, progressing towards Final Investment Decision by year-end 2025. Highest lithium brine grade recorded: 616 mg/L, with average 582 mg/L in Lester well. AOGC approved 2.5% royalty rate.
data.description: Standard Lithium Ltd. announced significant progress in the development of the South West Arkansas (SWA) Project, aiming for a Final Investment Decision by the end of 2025. The company completed all fieldwork for Phase 1 of the SWA Project, targeting a production capacity of 22,500 tonnes per annum of battery-quality lithium carbonate. The latest exploration well, Lester well, revealed the highest lithium concentration in the SWA Project area at 616 mg/L lithium in brine. Additionally, the Arkansas Oil and Gas Commission approved a 2.5% royalty rate for Phase I of the SWA Project, setting a precedent for lithium development companies in the region. By the end of the third quarter, Standard Lithium plans to release a Definitive Feasibility Study for SWA and a Maiden Inferred Resource Report for their East Texas properties, further solidifying the project's potential value. These developments demonstrate the company's commitment to advancing their projects and securing critical minerals production in the United States.

data.title: Global patient handling equipment market to reach $16.4 billion by 2030, growing at a CAGR of 5.8%; Europe leads the market.
data.description: The global patient handling equipment market has shown consistent growth, reaching a value of US$12.4 billion in 2025, from US$11.8 billion in 2024. It is estimated to have a CAGR of 5.8% from 2025 to 2030, aiming for a forecasted valuation of US$16.4 billion. This growth is attributed to the increasing demand for efficient patient mobility solutions due to a rise in chronic illnesses, an aging population, and obesity rates. Key factors driving this growth include the rising prevalence of neurological and cardiovascular conditions, expansion of elderly care facilities, emphasis on reducing caregiver injuries, growth of home healthcare services, and integration of AI in medical devices. The largest market segment is patient transfer devices, such as lifts and transfer mattresses, due to the focus on safety and comfort during patient mobility. Europe leads the market, driven by its aging population and strong healthcare infrastructure, along with the presence of key global manufacturers.
